evals.prompt: Set up a live alert that POSTs to my OpenCLAW webhook (https://gw.example/hooks/agent) whenever BTC RSI drops below 30 AND funding is above 0.5% on the 1-minute bar. Don't spam — at most one fire every 15 minutes.
evals.expected_output: Complete strategy JSON with one indicator-driven evaluator, gateMode 'all', rsi op '<' value 30, fundingRate op '>' value 0.5, on bar_close.BTC-USD-PERP, cooldownAfterFire '15m', webhook url and tokenEnv set. Includes uv run command to launch the listener.

assertions.name: valid_json_block
assertions.description: Response includes a complete strategy JSON with required top-level fields name, underlying, subscriptions, evaluators

assertions.name: subscriptions_include_bbo_and_funding
assertions.description: subscriptions.market includes both a BTC BBO or trades channel AND a funding channel for BTC-USD-PERP

assertions.name: rsi_under_30
assertions.description: evaluators[0].conditions.rsi has enabled true, op '<', value 30

assertions.name: funding_above_threshold
assertions.description: evaluators[0].conditions.fundingRate has enabled true, op '>', value of 0.5 (or 0.005 if expressed as decimal)

assertions.name: gate_mode_all
assertions.description: evaluators[0].conditions.gateMode is 'all' (both conditions must hold)

assertions.name: cooldown_15m
assertions.description: evaluators[0].cooldownAfterFire is '15m' (or equivalent expression of 15 minutes)

assertions.name: openclaw_url_present
assertions.description: evaluators[0].webhook.url contains the user's https://gw.example/hooks/agent URL exactly as given

assertions.name: token_env_referenced
assertions.description: webhook config references a tokenEnv (default OPENCLAW_TOKEN) and the response notes the user must export it before running

assertions.name: run_command_included
assertions.description: Response shows the uv run scripts/paradex_listener.py command to launch the listener (mentions --dry-run for safe smoke test)

evals.prompt: I want to mirror every BTC perp BUY fill above 0.1 BTC to my OpenCLAW webhook. The hook URL is https://gw.example/hooks/wake and my token is in OPENCLAW_TOKEN.
evals.expected_output: Strategy JSON with subscriptions.user including 'fills', one evaluator using 'match' (not 'conditions') with market BTC-USD-PERP, side BUY, minSize 0.1, on ['fills'], webhook URL and tokenEnv. Response notes auth env vars (PARADEX_ACCOUNT_PRIVATE_KEY, PARADEX_L1_ADDRESS) are required because user channels are in use.

assertions.name: user_channel_fills
assertions.description: subscriptions.user includes 'fills'

assertions.name: uses_match_not_conditions
assertions.description: evaluators[0] has a 'match' block and does NOT have a 'conditions' block (event-driven, not indicator-driven)

assertions.name: match_market_side_size
assertions.description: match block has market 'BTC-USD-PERP', side 'BUY', minSize 0.1

assertions.name: on_fills
assertions.description: evaluators[0].on contains 'fills'

assertions.name: webhook_url_correct
assertions.description: webhook.url is exactly https://gw.example/hooks/wake

assertions.name: auth_env_warning
assertions.description: Response mentions that user channels (subscriptions.user) require auth — at least one of PARADEX_JWT_TOKEN OR (PARADEX_ACCOUNT_PRIVATE_KEY + PARADEX_L1_ADDRESS) env vars

evals.prompt: Monitor BTC for me — fire when the 1m close drops below the 24h SMA AND RSI is under 35. Send to https://gw.example/hooks/agent. I have a listener running with --watch on strategies/.
evals.expected_output: Strategy JSON saved as strategies/<descriptive-name>.json with subscriptions including trades.BTC-USD-PERP, one indicator-driven evaluator with sma op 'below' period 24, rsi op '<' value 35, gateMode 'all', on bar_close.BTC-USD-PERP, webhook URL exactly as given. Response explains that the listener will pick up the file within ~2s of write (watch interval) without restart, and confirms the file was placed in strategies/.

assertions.name: valid_strategy_json
assertions.description: Response generates a complete strategy JSON with name, underlying BTC, subscriptions, evaluators

assertions.name: sma_below_condition
assertions.description: Strategy contains an SMA-below-24h condition. Accepted forms: legacy {conditions.sma: {enabled: true, op: 'below', period: 24}} (1h bars), or new {expression: {... op: 'below'/'<', lhs: {event: 'close'}, rhs: {indicator: 'sma', period: P}}} where P represents 24 hours given the chosen barSize (period: 24 at 1h bars, period: 1440 at 1m bars, period: 96 at 15m bars).

assertions.name: rsi_under_35
assertions.description: Strategy contains an RSI < 35 condition, in either form: legacy {conditions.rsi: {enabled: true, op: '<', value: 35}}, or new expression {op: '<', lhs: {indicator: 'rsi'}, rhs: {const: 35}}.

assertions.name: gate_combines_with_and
assertions.description: The SMA-below and RSI<35 conditions are combined with AND semantics (both must hold). Accepted: legacy conditions.gateMode 'all', or new expression with an 'all' combinator wrapping both comparisons.

assertions.name: on_bar_close
assertions.description: evaluators[0].on contains bar_close.BTC-USD-PERP (synthetic event from trades or bbo)

assertions.name: webhook_url_exact
assertions.description: evaluators[0].webhook.url is exactly https://gw.example/hooks/agent

assertions.name: submit_to_watched_dir
assertions.description: Response saves the JSON into strategies/ directory (or instructs the user to do so) with a descriptive filename like btc-sma-rsi.json
assertions.section: Workflow D: Submit a strategy to a running listener

assertions.name: explains_hot_reload
assertions.description: Response explains that --watch mode will hot-reload the strategy within a few seconds without restarting the listener
assertions.section: Workflow D: Submit a strategy to a running listener

evals.prompt: Watch ETH funding — alert me whenever it flips negative for any reason, day or night.
evals.expected_output: Strategy JSON written to strategies/ with subscriptions.market including funding.ETH-USD-PERP, one evaluator with conditions.fundingRate.op '<' value 0, gateMode 'any', on bar_close.ETH-USD-PERP, sensible cooldownAfterFire (e.g. 1h to avoid spam on funding ticks). Response provides the run command if no listener is running, or confirms the strategy was dropped into the watched directory if one is.

assertions.name: eth_funding_subscription
assertions.description: subscriptions.market includes funding.ETH-USD-PERP

assertions.name: funding_negative_threshold
assertions.description: evaluators[0].conditions.fundingRate has op '<' and value 0 (or 0.0)

assertions.name: any_or_single_condition
assertions.description: The gate fires whenever funding is negative — funding is the only enabled condition, OR gateMode is 'any', OR the expression is a single comparison. (gateMode 'all' with a single condition also passes since semantics are equivalent.)

assertions.name: cooldown_present
assertions.description: evaluator has a cooldownAfterFire OR throttle of at least 5 minutes to avoid spam — funding updates ~3x per day so any non-trivial cooldown qualifies

assertions.name: submit_or_run_command
assertions.description: Response either drops the JSON into strategies/ for a watching listener OR provides the uv run command with --watch documented
assertions.section: Workflow D: Submit a strategy to a running listener

evals.prompt: Set up monitoring on three things at once — BTC oversold (RSI<30), ETH overbought (RSI>70), and SOL whale fills above 10 SOL. Same OpenCLAW hook for all: https://gw.example/hooks/agent.
evals.expected_output: Three strategy JSONs (or one strategy with three evaluators), each saved as a separate file in strategies/ (e.g. btc-oversold.json, eth-overbought.json, sol-whale.json). The whale-fill strategy uses 'match' not 'conditions', subscribes to user fills, and notes the auth env var requirement. Response lists all files written and confirms how the watching listener will load them.

assertions.name: three_distinct_strategies
assertions.description: Response generates 3 separate strategies (or one with 3 evaluators) — covering BTC RSI<30, ETH RSI>70, SOL whale fill

assertions.name: btc_rsi_oversold
assertions.description: BTC strategy has rsi op '<' value 30 with on bar_close.BTC-USD-PERP

assertions.name: eth_rsi_overbought
assertions.description: ETH strategy has rsi op '>' value 70 with on bar_close.ETH-USD-PERP

assertions.name: sol_whale_uses_match
assertions.description: SOL whale strategy uses 'match' (not conditions), with market SOL-USD-PERP, minSize 10, on ['fills']

assertions.name: user_channel_auth_warning
assertions.description: Response notes the SOL whale fills strategy requires Paradex auth because it uses subscriptions.user — at least one of PARADEX_JWT_TOKEN OR (PARADEX_ACCOUNT_PRIVATE_KEY + PARADEX_L1_ADDRESS) must be set

assertions.name: all_use_same_url
assertions.description: All three strategies have webhook.url == https://gw.example/hooks/agent

evals.prompt: What's the difference between paradex-strategy-listener and paradex-strategy-backtester? When should I use which?
evals.expected_output: Clear explanation that backtester replays historical bars to validate a strategy offline, listener subscribes to live WS feeds and POSTs webhooks on real-time triggers; same gate schema (rsi/sma/rvPctile/ivPctile/fundingRate) so a backtested strategy can be promoted to a live alert. Listener does not place orders — it only generates signals via OpenCLAW.

assertions.name: explains_backtester_offline
assertions.description: Response describes backtester as offline / historical / simulation

assertions.name: explains_listener_realtime
assertions.description: Response describes listener as real-time / live / WS-subscribed and mentions webhook fanout

assertions.name: shared_gate_schema
assertions.description: Response notes the two skills share the same indicator gate schema (rsi/sma/rvPctile/ivPctile/fundingRate, gateMode all/any/min)

assertions.name: listener_no_orders
assertions.description: Response makes clear the listener does NOT place orders — it generates signals to OpenCLAW only

assertions.name: promotion_workflow
assertions.description: Response mentions integration with at least one sibling skill: that listener can deploy a strategy validated by paradex-strategy-backtester and/or designed by paradex-strategy-builder
assertions.section: Workflow A: Backtested strategy → live listener

trigger_evals.query: alert me when BTC RSI drops below 30
POST to my OpenCLAW webhook when funding flips positive
set up a real-time strategy listener for ETH
mirror my fills to a webhook
subscribe to BTC BBO and fire when spread widens
deploy this backtested strategy as a live alert
watch the market and trigger a webhook when conditions are met
monitor BTC and tell me when it drops below the 24h average
monitor ETH funding rate, alert if it goes negative
watch for whale fills on SOL — anything above 50 SOL
keep an eye on BTC and ping me on RSI extremes
set up monitoring for a breakout above the 20-bar high
I want to be notified when ETH crosses below its SMA50
track BTC and fire when volume spikes 3x the average
submit this strategy to my running listener
drop a new alert into the watched strategies directory
backtest a short strangle on BTC
what is the current funding rate
show me my open positions
calculate the delta of this option
what's the margin utilisation of my account
scan vaults by sharpe ratio

trigger_evals_validation.query: fire a webhook when RSI crosses 30
monitor my account for liquidation risk and ping the hook
watch BTC — fire when funding flips negative
alert me on every ETH fill above 5 ETH
what was my P&L last week
